generatedSentence: Our junior sailor, partway through an experiment on other-worldly guppy suckers, often found himself gazing at the ordinary raindrops outside, pondering if there was an outsize option for a life beyond the caisson. He'd speak of moderate miracles, hoping kismet would intervene. Despite the local hype, his unbiased opinion was that the hardware to giddyup and kill the monotony needed a revolutionary overture, but he didn't want to vaseline over the reality, having once whacked his head on a pipe. A profound aftershock followed the homecoming of other-worldly visitors, cloaked in a shimmering veil. Their presence could petrify the toughest nails. Their miniature huskie, an edible turkey-corn hybrid, had a curious sting. A mockingbird, usually helpful, offered a languid opinion. The newsman would expound how we must adapt and adjudge their intentions, but their bejeweled recording only made them seem crackers, like a giant ladybug, not flirty, just factitious. The anaconda-sized obelisk, once a peaceful cradle, began flashing with immense sonic energy, jackknifing towards the hotels and dumpster. The keyholder, a worried geologist, knew these revolutionary visitors, the multitude of pranksters and knockabout ichthyosaur pilots, intended to gerrymander our trust. He deftly reduced the tractor's output, his guitar's ribbon fluttering, knowing one fragile unitary mistake could mean kill, as the cutlass-wielding visitors started to beleaguer the pool. He watched, an unbiased newsman reporting, as the jackrabbit-quick sailor, using nailbrushes and a jug of varnish, managed to arbitrate the situation, coaxing the visitors to reduce their outsize hype. They shared cinnamon toast and edible corn, pricked by the rabbitbrush but ultimately agreeing to a widescreen treaty. The aftershock lessened, a profound peace descending. He knew this was a moderate miracle, a true homecoming, as the other-worldly visitors departed, leaving only a feather and a letterbox full of thanks.

Despite the local hype, his unbiased opinion was that the hardware to giddyup and kill the monotony needed a revolutionary overture, but he didn't want to vaseline over the reality, having once whacked his head on a pipe.

A profound aftershock followed the homecoming of other-worldly visitors, cloaked in a shimmering veil. Their presence could petrify the toughest nails. Their miniature huskie, an edible turkey-corn hybrid, had a curious sting. A mockingbird, usually helpful, offered a languid opinion. The newsman would expound how we must adapt and adjudge their intentions, but their bejeweled recording only made them seem crackers, like a giant ladybug, not flirty, just factitious.

The anaconda-sized obelisk, once a peaceful cradle, began flashing with immense sonic energy, jackknifing towards the hotels and dumpster. The keyholder, a worried geologist, knew these revolutionary visitors, the multitude of pranksters and knockabout ichthyosaur pilots, intended to gerrymander our trust. He deftly reduced the tractor's output, his guitar's ribbon fluttering, knowing one fragile unitary mistake could mean kill, as the cutlass-wielding visitors started to beleaguer the pool.

rawPanelSentences: Our junior sailor, partway through an experiment on other-worldly guppy suckers, often found himself gazing at the ordinary raindrops outside, pondering if there was an outsize option for a life beyond the caisson. He'd speak of moderate miracles, hoping kismet would intervene. Despite the local hype, his unbiased opinion was that the hardware to giddyup and kill the monotony needed a revolutionary overture, but he didn't want to vaseline over the reality, having once whacked his head on a pipe.

A profound aftershock followed the homecoming of other-worldly visitors, cloaked in a shimmering veil. Their presence could petrify the toughest nails. Their miniature huskie, an edible turkey-corn hybrid, had a curious sting. A mockingbird, usually helpful, offered a languid opinion. The newsman would expound how we must adapt and adjudge their intentions, but their bejeweled recording only made them seem crackers, like a giant ladybug, not flirty, just factitious.

The anaconda-sized obelisk, once a peaceful cradle, began flashing with immense sonic energy, jackknifing towards the hotels and dumpster. The keyholder, a worried geologist, knew these revolutionary visitors, the multitude of pranksters and knockabout ichthyosaur pilots, intended to gerrymander our trust. He deftly reduced the tractor's output, his guitar's ribbon fluttering, knowing one fragile unitary mistake could mean kill, as the cutlass-wielding visitors started to beleaguer the pool.

He watched, an unbiased newsman reporting, as the jackrabbit-quick sailor, using nailbrushes and a jug of varnish, managed to arbitrate the situation, coaxing the visitors to reduce their outsize hype. They shared cinnamon toast and edible corn, pricked by the rabbitbrush but ultimately agreeing to a widescreen treaty. The aftershock lessened, a profound peace descending. He knew this was a moderate miracle, a true homecoming, as the other-worldly visitors departed, leaving only a feather and a letterbox full of thanks.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A young male sailor with short cropped hair, wearing a retro-futuristic navy uniform with glowing cyan accents and a polished brass telescope attached to his belt. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The sailor looks out a rain-streaked porthole of a futuristic caisson, a miniature other-worldly guppy tank glowing beside him, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Other-worldly visitors, cloaked in shimmering veils, stand beside a miniature huskie, as a distressed mockingbird flies overhead, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: An immense, flashing obelisk-like structure begins to jackknife towards distant futuristic hotels, a geologist with a guitar watches in concern, close-up on geologist's face. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The sailor offers cinnamon toast and a jug to one of the alien visitors, both sitting peacefully by a futuristic pool, a feather gently floats down, wide shot.

definitions.definition: One of a set of choices that can be made.
The freedom or right to choose.

A contract giving the holder the right to buy or sell an asset at a set strike price; can apply to financial market transactions, or to ordinary transactions for tangible assets such as a residence or automobile.
To purchase an option on something.
definitions.example: The new novel was optioned by the film studio, but they'll probably never decide to make a movie from it.

definitions.definition: To configure, by setting an option.
Of, concerned with, or preoccupied with a different world than that of the tangible here and now, such as a heavenly, spiritual, or imaginary world.
Not belonging to the real world; unnatural; odd and unfamiliar.
A single droplet of rainwater that has just fallen or is falling from the sky.
To some extent.
In part.
A low class bar, a speakeasy.
Language, jargon, or terminology used uniquely in a particular environment or group.
definitions.example: Corporate speak; IT speak.

definitions.definition: Speech, conversation.
To communicate with one's voice, to say words out loud.
definitions.example: I was so surprised I couldn't speak.

definitions.definition: To have a conversation.
definitions.example: It's been ages since we've spoken.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To communicate or converse by some means other than orally, such as writing or facial expressions.
definitions.example: Actions speak louder than words.

definitions.definition: To deliver a message to a group; to deliver a speech.
definitions.example: This evening I shall speak on the topic of correct English usage.

definitions.definition: To be able to communicate in a language.
definitions.example: He speaks Mandarin fluently.

definitions.definition: To utter.
definitions.example: I was so surprised that I couldn't speak a word.

definitions.definition: To communicate (some fact or feeling); to bespeak, to indicate.
(sometimes humorous) To understand (as though it were a language).
definitions.example: So you can program in C. But do you speak C++?

definitions.definition: To produce a sound; to sound.
Of a bird, to be able to vocally reproduce words or phrases from a human language.
To address; to accost; to speak to.
Petroleum jelly.
Any particular kind of petroleum jelly or of any similar lubricant.
A greenish-yellow coloured glass, or the colouring used in the manufacture of this glass.
To lubricate with vaseline.
definitions.example: Even at this late date, vaselining will preserve the best of these leathers.

definitions.definition: To hit, slap or strike.
To kill, bump off.
To share or parcel out; often with up.
definitions.example: to whack the spoils of a robbery

definitions.definition: To beat convincingly; to thrash.
(usually in the negative) To surpass; to better.
Tired; fatigued.
definitions.example: After that all-night party, we were all whacked.

definitions.definition: Intoxicated on drugs.
Impartial or without bias or prejudice.
A tiny freshwater fish, Poecilia reticulata, popular in home aquariums, that usually has a plain body and black or dark blue tail for the females and a more colorful tail for the males.
definitions.example: Fred kept a tank of guppies by the windowsill.

definitions.definition: By extension, any tiny fish.
A tube holding paintballs before they are loaded into the gun.
definitions.example: 2001, John R. Little, Curtis Wong, Ultimate Paintball Field Guide (page 177)

definitions.definition: A younger person.
definitions.example: four years his junior

definitions.definition: A name suffix used after a son's name when his father has the same name (abbreviations: Jnr., Jr., Jun.).
A third-year student at a high school or university.
A junior barrister.
Low in rank; having a subordinate role, job, or situation.
(often preceded by a possessive adjective or a possessive form of a noun) Younger.
Belonging to a younger person, or an earlier time of life.
Of or pertaining to a third academic year in a four-year high school (eleventh grade) or university.
Promotion or propaganda; especially exaggerated claims.
definitions.example: After all the hype for the diet plan, only the results ended up slim.

definitions.definition: To promote heavily; to advertise or build up.
definitions.example: They started hyping the new magazine months before its release.

definitions.definition: Fate; a predetermined or unavoidable destiny.
An unusually large garment size
To exceed in size
Of an unusually large size
A person with authority; authority, ordinance.
Something ordinary or regular.
(of a judge) Having regular jurisdiction; now only used in certain phrases.
Being part of the natural order of things; normal, customary, routine.
definitions.example: On an ordinary day I wake up at nine o'clock, work for six hours, and then go to the gym.

definitions.definition: Having no special characteristics or function; everyday, common, mundane; often deprecatory.
definitions.example: He looked so ordinary, I never thought he'd be capable of murder.

definitions.definition: Bad or undesirable.
To be very happy, be delighted, exult; to feel joy.
To have (someone) as a lover or spouse; to enjoy sexually.
To make happy, exhilarate.
To enjoy.
An enclosure from which water can be expelled, in order to give access to underwater areas for engineering works etc.
The gate across the entrance to a dry dock.
A floating tank that can be submerged, attached to an underwater object and then pumped out to lift the object by buoyancy; a camel.
A two-wheeled, horse-drawn military vehicle used to carry ammunition (and a coffin at funerals).
A large box to hold ammunition.
A chest filled with explosive materials, used like a mine.
A coffer.
A person in the business of navigating ships or other vessels
Someone knowledgeable in the practical management of ships.
definitions.example: He's a talented sailor and has spent many years at sea.

definitions.definition: A member of the crew of a vessel; a mariner; a common seaman.
A person who sails sailing boats as a sport or recreation.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genera Neptis, Pseudoneptis and Phaedyma, having white markings on a dark base and commonly flying by gliding.
A person or animal that sucks, especially a breast or udder; especially a suckling animal, young mammal before it is weaned.
An undesired stem growing out of the roots or lower trunk of a shrub or tree, especially from the rootstock of a grafted plant or tree.
(by extension) A parasite; a sponger.
An organ or body part that does the sucking; especially a round structure on the bodies of some insects, frogs, and octopuses that allows them to stick to surfaces.
A thing that works by sucking something.
The embolus, or bucket, of a pump; also, the valve of a pump basket.
A pipe through which anything is drawn.

A small piece of leather, usually round, having a string attached to the center, which, when saturated with water and pressed upon a stone or other body having a smooth surface, adheres, by reason of the atmospheric pressure, with such force as to enable a considerable weight to be thus lifted by the string; formerly used by children as a plaything.
A suction cup.
An animal such as the octopus and remora, which adhere to other bodies with such organs.
Any fish in the family Catostomidae of North America and eastern Asia, which have mouths modified into downward-pointing, suckerlike structures for feeding in bottom sediments.
A lollipop; a piece of candy which is sucked.
A hard drinker.
An inhabitant of Illinois.

A migrant lead miner working in the Driftless Area of northwest Illinois, southwest Wisconsin, and northeast Iowa, working in summer and leaving for winter, so named because of the similarity to the migratory patterns of the North American Catostomidae.
A person who is easily deceived, tricked or persuaded to do something; a naive person.
definitions.example: One poor sucker had actually given her his life’s savings.

definitions.definition: A person irresistibly attracted by something specified.
definitions.example: A sucker for ghost stories.

definitions.definition: (British slang) The penis.
Any thing or object.
definitions.example: See if you can get that sucker working again.

definitions.definition: A person.
A test under controlled conditions made to either demonstrate a known truth, examine the validity of a hypothesis, or determine the efficacy of something previously untried.
Experience, practical familiarity with something.
To conduct an experiment.
To experience; to feel; to perceive; to detect.
To test or ascertain by experiment; to try out; to make an experiment on.
To cause a horse or similar mount to speed up.
(by extension) To start moving or move faster; to get a move on.
(directed at a horse) Move on!, go faster!
One who holds an intermediate position between extremes, as in politics.
definitions.example: The moderates are the natural advocates of ecumenism against the fanatics of their churches.

definitions.definition: One of a party in Scottish Church history dominant in the 18th century, lax in doctrine and discipline, but intolerant of evangelicalism and popular rights. It caused the secessions of 1733 and 1761, and its final resultant was the Disruption of 1843.
To reduce the excessiveness of (something)
definitions.example: to moderate rage, action, desires, etc.

definitions.definition: To become less excessive
To preside over (something) as a moderator
definitions.example: to moderate a synod

definitions.definition: To act as a moderator; to assist in bringing to compromise
Not excessive; acting in moderation
definitions.example: a moderate Calvinist

definitions.definition: Mediocre
Average priced; standard-deal
Not violent or rigorous; temperate; mild; gentle.
definitions.example: a moderate winter

definitions.definition: Having an intermediate position between liberal and conservative.
An event that appears inexplicable by the laws of nature and so is held to be supernatural in origin.
definitions.example: An example of a miracle associated with Muhammad is the splitting of the moon.

definitions.definition: A fortunate outcome that prevails despite overwhelming odds against it.
An awesome and exceptional example of something
To affect by a miracle; to work a miracle upon.

Fixtures, equipment, tools and devices used for general-purpose construction and repair of a structure or object. Also such equipment as sold as stock by a store of the same name, e.g. hardware store.
Equipment.
definitions.example: military hardware

definitions.definition: The part of a computer that is fixed and cannot be altered without replacement or physical modification; motherboard, expansion cards, etc. Compare software.
Electronic equipment.
Metal implements.
A firearm.
The act of killing.
definitions.example: The assassin liked to make a clean kill, and thus favored small arms over explosives.

definitions.definition: Specifically, the death blow.
definitions.example: The hunter delivered the kill with a pistol shot to the head.

definitions.definition: The result of killing; that which has been killed.
definitions.example: The fox dragged its kill back to its den.

definitions.definition: The grounding of the ball on the opponent's court, winning the rally.
To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
definitions.example: Smoking kills more people each year than alcohol and drugs combined.

definitions.definition: To render inoperative.
definitions.example: He killed the engine and turned off the headlights, but remained in the car, waiting.

definitions.definition: To stop, cease or render void; to terminate.
definitions.example: My computer wouldn't respond until I killed some of the running processes.

definitions.definition: To amaze, exceed, stun or otherwise incapacitate.
definitions.example: That joke always kills me.

definitions.definition: To cause great pain, discomfort or distress to.
definitions.example: These tight shoes are killing my feet.

definitions.definition: To produce feelings of dissatisfaction or revulsion in.
definitions.example: It kills me to learn how many poor people are practically starving in this country while rich moguls spend such outrageous amounts on useless luxuries.

definitions.definition: To use up or to waste.
definitions.example: He told the bartender, pointing at the bottle of scotch he planned to consume, "Leave it, I'm going to kill the bottle."

definitions.definition: To exert an overwhelming effect on.
definitions.example: Between the two of us, we killed the rest of the case of beer.

definitions.definition: To overpower, overwhelm or defeat.
definitions.example: The team had absolutely killed their traditional rivals, and the local sports bars were raucous with celebrations.

definitions.definition: To force a company out of business.
To produce intense pain.
definitions.example: You don't ever want to get rabies. The doctor will have to give you multiple shots and they really kill.

definitions.definition: To punish severely.
definitions.example: My parents are going to kill me!

definitions.definition: To strike (a ball, etc.) with such force and placement as to make a shot that is impossible to defend against, usually winning a point.
To cause (a ball, etc.) to be out of play, resulting in a stoppage of gameplay.
To succeed with an audience, especially in comedy.
To cause to assume the value zero.
(IRC) To disconnect (a user) involuntarily from the network.
To deadmelt.
An opening; a recess or chamber.
Disclosure; discovery; revelation.

A motion placed before a legislative body, such as the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland.
A musical introduction to a piece of music.
To make overtures; to approach with a proposal.
Any of the Coccinellidae family of beetles, typically having a round shape and red or yellow spotted elytra.
The deep; the sea; the ocean.
An abyss.
To cause to sink deeply; to cause to dive or penetrate far down.
To dive deeply; to penetrate.
Descending far below the surface; opening or reaching to great depth; deep.
Very deep; very serious
Intellectually deep; entering far into subjects; reaching to the bottom of a matter, or of a branch of learning; thorough
definitions.example: a profound investigation

definitions.definition: Characterized by intensity; deeply felt; pervading
Bending low, exhibiting or expressing deep humility; lowly; submissive
To harden organic matter by permeating with water and depositing dissolved minerals.
To produce rigidity akin to stone.
To immobilize with fright.
To become stone, or of a stony hardness, as organic matter by calcareous deposits.
To become stony, callous, or obdurate.
To make callous or obdurate; to stupefy; to paralyze; to transform; as by petrification.
A dry, thin, crispy baked bread (usually salty or savoury, but sometimes sweet, as in the case of graham crackers and animal crackers).
A short piece of twisted string tied to the end of a whip that creates the distinctive sound when the whip is thrown or cracked.
A firecracker.
A person or thing that cracks, or that cracks a thing (e.g. whip cracker; nutcracker).
A Christmas cracker.
Refinery equipment used to pyrolyse organic feedstocks. If catalyst is used to aid pyrolysis it is informally called a cat-cracker
A fine thing or person (crackerjack).
definitions.example: She's an absolute cracker!

definitions.definition: An ambitious or hard-working person (i.e. someone who arises at the 'crack' of dawn).
One who cracks (i.e. overcomes) computer software or security restrictions.
A noisy boaster; a swaggering fellow.
(racial slur) An impoverished white person from the southeastern United States, originally associated with Georgia and parts of Florida; (by extension) any white person.
(Florida) A police officer.
A northern pintail, species of dabbling duck.
A pair of fluted rolls for grinding caoutchouc.
Crazy, insane.
Something hung up or spread out to hide or protect the face, or hide an object from view; usually of gauze, crepe, or similar diaphanous material.
A cover; disguise; a mask; a pretense.
The calyptra of mosses.
A membrane connecting the margin of the pileus of a mushroom with the stalk; a velum.
A covering for a person or thing; as, a caul (especially over the head)

definitions.definition: Velum (A circular membrane round the cap of medusa)
A thin layer of tissue which is attached to or covers a mushroom.
An obscuration of the clearness of the tones in pronunciation.
To dress in, or decorate with, a veil.
To conceal as with a veil.
definitions.example: The forest fire was veiled by smoke, but I could hear it clearly.

definitions.definition: Someone who visits someone else; someone staying as a guest.
Someone who pays a visit to a specific place or event; a sightseer or tourist.
(usually in the plural) Someone, or a team, that is playing away from home.
A person authorized to visit an institution to see that it is being managed properly.
An extraterrestrial being on Earth for any reason.
An object which lands or passes by Earth or its orbit.
A head or overseer of an institution such as a college (in which case, equivalent to the university's chancellor) or cathedral or hospital, who resolves disputes, gives ceremonial speeches, etc.
The object in the visitor pattern that performs an operation on the elements of a structure one by one.
To make suitable; to make to correspond; to fit or suit
To fit by alteration; to modify or remodel for a different purpose; to adjust

definitions.definition: To make by altering or fitting something else; to produce by change of form or character

definitions.definition: To make oneself comfortable to a new thing.
definitions.example: They could not adapt to the new climate and so perished.

definitions.definition: Adapted; fit; suited; suitable.
To declare to be.
To deem or determine to be.
To award judicially; to assign.
A long-tailed American songbird of the Mimidae family, noted for its ability to mimic calls of other birds.
Furnishing help; giving aid; useful.
Any of several breeds of dogs used as sled dogs.
A subjective belief, judgment or perspective that a person has formed about a topic, issue, person or thing.
definitions.example: Every man is a fool in some man's opinion.

definitions.definition: The judgment or sentiment which the mind forms of persons or things; estimation.
Favorable estimation; hence, consideration; reputation; fame; public sentiment or esteem.
Obstinacy in holding to one's belief or impression; opiniativeness; conceitedness.
The formal decision, or expression of views, of a judge, an umpire, a doctor, or other party officially called upon to consider and decide upon a matter or point submitted.
(European Union law) a judicial opinion delivered by an Advocate General to the European Court of Justice where he or she proposes a legal solution to the cases for which the court is responsible
To have or express as an opinion.
To decorate or bedeck with jewels or gems.
To make a record of information.
definitions.example: I wanted to record every detail of what happened, for the benefit of future generations.

definitions.definition: To make an audio or video recording of.
definitions.example: Within a week they had recorded both the song and the video for it.

definitions.definition: To give legal status to by making an official public record.
definitions.example: When the deed was recorded, we officially owned the house.

definitions.definition: To fix in a medium, usually in a tangible medium.
To make an audio, video, or multimedia recording.
To repeat; to practice.
To sing or repeat a tune.
To reflect; to ponder.
A reproduction of sound, video, etc. stored in a permanent medium.
definitions.example: I made a recording of the TV show so that I could watch it later.

definitions.definition: A person who obtains things by force; a thug or bully.
definitions.example: They were doing fine until they encountered a bunch of toughs from the opposition.

definitions.definition: To endure.
To toughen.
Strong and resilient; sturdy.
definitions.example: The tent, made of tough canvas, held up to many abuses.

definitions.definition: (of food) Difficult to cut or chew.
definitions.example: To soften a tough cut of meat, the recipe suggested simmering it for hours.

definitions.definition: Rugged or physically hardy.
definitions.example: Only a tough species will survive in the desert.

definitions.definition: Stubborn.
definitions.example: He had a reputation as a tough negotiator.

definitions.definition: (of weather etc) Harsh or severe.
Rowdy or rough.

definitions.definition: (of questions, etc.) Difficult or demanding.
definitions.example: This is a tough crowd.

definitions.definition: Undergoing plastic deformation before breaking.
Used to indicate lack of sympathy

definitions.definition: The thin, horny plate at the ends of fingers and toes on humans and some other animals.
definitions.example: When I'm nervous I bite my nails.

definitions.definition: The basal thickened portion of the anterior wings of certain hemiptera.
The terminal horny plate on the beak of ducks, and other allied birds.
The claw of a bird or other animal.

A spike-shaped metal fastener used for joining wood or similar materials. The nail is generally driven through two or more layers of material by means of impacts from a hammer or other device. It is then held in place by friction.
A round pedestal on which merchants once carried out their business, such as the four nails outside The Exchange, Bristol.
An archaic English unit of length equivalent to 1/20 of an ell or 1/16 of a yard (2 1/4 inches or 5.715 cm).
To fix (an object) to another object using a nail.

definitions.definition: To drive a nail.

definitions.definition: To stud or boss with nails, or as if with nails.
To catch.
To expose as a sham.
To accomplish (a task) completely and successfully.
definitions.example: I really nailed that test.

definitions.definition: To hit (a target) effectively with some weapon.
Of a male, to engage in sexual intercourse with.
To spike, as a cannon.
To nail down: to make certain, or confirm.
An earthquake that follows in the same vicinity as another, usually larger, earthquake (the "mainshock").
By extension, any result or consequence following a major event.
Emotional and physical distress following a traumatic event.
Greatly diminished size or form; reduced scale.
A small version of something; a model of reduced scale.
definitions.example: There was a miniature of a whaling ship in a glass bottle over the mantlepiece.

definitions.definition: A small, highly detailed painting, a portrait miniature.
The art of painting such highly detailed miniature works.
An illustration in an illuminated manuscript.
A musical composition which is short in duration.
definitions.example: Sacha composed a miniature for strings as a final project at the conservatory.

definitions.definition: A chess game which is concluded with very few moves.
A token in a game representing a unit or character.
definitions.example: Jack had dozens of miniatures of Napoleonic footsoldiers painted in detailed period regalia for his wargames.

definitions.definition: Lettering in red; rubric distinction.
A particular feature or trait.
To make smaller than normal; to reproduce in miniature.
Smaller than normal.
definitions.example: I find miniature dogs annoying; they seem to yap more than full-size dogs.

definitions.definition: The act or event of returning home.
definitions.example: His homecoming was saddened by the news of his mother's death the day before.

definitions.definition: In colleges and high schools, a tradition centred around a football game, a parade and the "coronation" of a Homecoming Queen.
definitions.example: Many alumni come back for homecoming, and many freshmen are advised to flee.

definitions.definition: Lacking enthusiasm, energy, or strength; drooping or flagging from weakness, fatigue, or lack of energy
definitions.example: languid breathing

definitions.definition: Heavy; dull; dragging; wanting spirit or animation; listless; apathetic.
The main cereal plant grown for its grain in a given region, such as oats in parts of Scotland and Ireland, and wheat or barley in England and Wales.
Maize, a grain crop of the species Zea mays.
A grain or seed, especially of a cereal crop.
definitions.example: He paid her the nominal fee of two corns of barley.

definitions.definition: A small, hard particle.
To granulate; to form a substance into grains
definitions.example: to corn gunpowder

definitions.definition: To preserve using coarse salt, e.g. corned beef
To provide with corn (typically maize; or, in Scotland, oats) for feed
definitions.example: Corn the horses.

definitions.definition: To render intoxicated
definitions.example: ale strong enough to corn one

definitions.definition: The guinea fowl (family Numididae).
A bird in the genus Meleagris with a fan-shaped tail and wattled neck, especially the wild turkey (Meleagris gallopavo, now domesticated).
The flesh or meat of this bird eaten as food.
definitions.example: All week after Thanksgiving, I had turkey sandwiches for lunch.

definitions.definition: With a distinguishing word: a bird resembling the Meleagris gallopavo (for example, the bush turkey or brush turkey (Alectura lathami), and the water turkey (Anhinga anhinga)).
An act of throwing three strikes in a row.
A patient feigning symptoms; a person faking illness or injury; a malingerer.
A pack carried by a lumberman; a bindle; also, a large travel bag, a suitcase.
A failure.
definitions.example: That film was a turkey.

definitions.definition: (usually mildly) A foolish or inept person.
definitions.example: The turkey cut in front of me and then berated me for running into him.

definitions.definition: A bump left on the skin after having been stung.
A bite by an insect.
A pointed portion of an insect or arachnid used for attack.
A sharp, localised pain primarily on the epidermis
A sharp-pointed hollow hair seated on a gland which secretes an acrid fluid, as in nettles.
The thrust of a sting into the flesh; the act of stinging; a wound inflicted by stinging.
(law enforcement) A police operation in which the police pretend to be criminals in order to catch a criminal.
A short percussive phrase played by a drummer to accent the punchline in a comedy show.
A brief sequence of music used in films, TV, and video games as a form of scenic punctuation or to identify the broadcasting station.
A support for a wind tunnel model which extends parallel to the air flow.
The harmful or painful part of something.
A goad; incitement.
The concluding point of an epigram or other sarcastic saying.
To set out the meaning of; to explain or discuss at length
To make a statement, especially at length.
definitions.example: He expounded often on the dangers of the imperial presidency.

definitions.definition: Anything edible.
(marijuana) a foodstuff, usually a baked good, infused with tetrahydrocannabinol from cannabutter etc.
That can be eaten without harm; innocuous to humans; suitable for consumption.
definitions.example: edible fruit

definitions.definition: That can be eaten without disgust.
definitions.example: Although stale, the bread was edible.

definitions.definition: A branching, hair-like structure that grows on the bodies of birds, used for flight, swimming, protection and display.
Long hair on the lower legs of a dog or horse, especially a draft horse, notably the Clydesdale breed. Narrowly only the rear hair.
One of the fins or wings on the shaft of an arrow.
A longitudinal strip projecting from an object to strengthen it, or to enter a channel in another object and thereby prevent displacement sideways but permit motion lengthwise; a spline.
Kind; nature; species (from the proverbial phrase "birds of a feather").

One of the two shims of the three-piece stone-splitting tool known as plug and feather or plug and feathers; the feathers are placed in a borehole and then a wedge is driven between them, causing the stone to split.
The angular adjustment of an oar or paddle-wheel float, with reference to a horizontal axis, as it leaves or enters the water.
Anything petty or trifling; a whit or jot.
(in the plural) Partridges and pheasants, as opposed to rabbits and hares (called fur).
To cover or furnish with feathers.
To arrange in the manner or appearance of feathers.
definitions.example: The stylist feathered my hair.

definitions.definition: To rotate the oars while they are out of the water to reduce wind resistance.
To streamline the blades of an aircraft's propeller by rotating them perpendicular to the axis of the propeller when the engine is shut down so that the propeller does not windmill during flight.
definitions.example: After striking the bird, the pilot feathered the left, damaged engine’s propeller.

definitions.definition: To finely shave or bevel an edge.
To intergrade or blend the pixels of an image with those of a background or neighboring image.
To adorn, as with feathers; to fringe.
To render light as a feather; to give wings to.
To enrich; to exalt; to benefit.
To tread, as a cock.
To accidentally touch the cue ball with the tip of the cue when taking aim
A collection point for mail intended for onward delivery, a secure box or receptacle for this purpose.
A delivery point for mail, a box, compartment or slot for this purpose.
A hidden container that holds a logbook and rubber stamp, found by following clues.
To transfer a widescreen motion picture to home video formats while preserving the original aspect ratio, with the placing of black bars above and below the picture area.
To hunt for letterboxes (containers with logbook and rubber stamp) by following clues.
Transferred to home video formats while preserving the original aspect ratio, having black bars above and below the picture area.
A revolutionist; a person who revolts.
, Chopin's 'Revolutionary Etude' Op. 10 no. 12
Of or pertaining to a revolution in government; tending to, or promoting, revolution
definitions.example: revolutionary agitators

definitions.definition: Pertaining to something that portends of great change; overthrowing a standing mindset
definitions.example: a revolutionary new tool

definitions.definition: Pertaining to something that revolves
A wheeled vehicle that moves independently, with at least three wheels, powered mechanically, steered by a driver and mostly for personal transportation.
definitions.example: She drove her car to the mall.

definitions.definition: A wheeled vehicle, drawn by a horse or other animal; a chariot.
An unpowered unit in a railroad train.
definitions.example: The conductor coupled the cars to the locomotive.

definitions.definition: An individual vehicle, powered or unpowered, in a multiple unit.
definitions.example: The 11:10 to London was operated by a 4-car diesel multiple unit.

definitions.definition: A passenger-carrying unit in a subway or elevated train, whether powered or not.
definitions.example: From the frontmost car of the subway, he filmed the progress through the tunnel.

definitions.definition: A rough unit of quantity approximating the amount which would fill a railroad car.
definitions.example: We ordered five hundred cars of gypsum.

definitions.definition: The moving, load-carrying component of an elevator or other cable-drawn transport mechanism.
definitions.example: Fix the car of the express elevator - the door is sticking.

definitions.definition: The passenger-carrying portion of certain amusement park rides, such as Ferris wheels.
definitions.example: The most exciting part of riding a Ferris wheel is when your car goes over the top.

definitions.definition: The part of an airship, such as a balloon or dirigible, which houses the passengers and control apparatus.
A sliding fitting that runs along a track.
The aggregate of desirable characteristics of a car.
definitions.example: Buy now! You can get more car for your money.

definitions.definition: A floating perforated box for living fish.
A short sword with a curved blade, and a convex edge; once used by sailors when boarding an enemy ship.
A similarly shaped tool; a machete.
To cut back (vegetation) with a cutlass.
Confidence in or reliance on some person or quality.
definitions.example: He needs to regain her trust if he is ever going to win her back.

definitions.definition: Dependence upon something in the future; hope.
Confidence in the future payment for goods or services supplied; credit.
definitions.example: I was out of cash, but the landlady let me have it on trust.

definitions.definition: That which is committed or entrusted; something received in confidence; a charge.
That upon which confidence is reposed; ground of reliance; hope.
Trustworthiness, reliability.
The condition or obligation of one to whom anything is confided; responsible charge or office.
The confidence vested in a person who has legal ownership of a property to manage for the benefit of another.
definitions.example: I put the house into my sister's trust.

definitions.definition: An estate devised or granted in confidence that the devisee or grantee shall convey it, or dispose of the profits, at the will, or for the benefit, of another; an estate held for the use of another.
A group of businessmen or traders organised for mutual benefit to produce and distribute specific commodities or services, and managed by a central body of trustees.
Affirmation of the access rights of a user of a computer system.
To place confidence in; to rely on, to confide, or have faith, in.
definitions.example: We cannot trust anyone who deceives us.

definitions.definition: To give credence to; to believe; to credit.
To hope confidently; to believe (usually with a phrase or infinitive clause as the object)
definitions.example: I trust you have cleaned your room?

definitions.definition: To show confidence in a person by entrusting them with something.
To commit, as to one's care; to entrust.
To give credit to; to sell to upon credit, or in confidence of future payment.
definitions.example: Merchants and manufacturers trust their customers annually with goods.

definitions.definition: (followed by to) To rely on (something), as though having trust (on it).
definitions.example: Having lost the book, he had to trust to his memory for further details.

definitions.definition: To risk; to venture confidently.
To have trust; to be credulous; to be won to confidence; to confide.
To sell or deliver anything in reliance upon a promise of payment; to give credit.
Secure, safe.
Faithful, dependable.
Of or relating to a trust.
A screen with a wider aspect ratio than the ordinary 35-millimeter frame, making more effective use of the human field of view and producing a more immersive view experience.
Filmed in a greater aspect ratio than the 1.33:1 or 1.37:1 aspect ratio.
(of a motion picture) Presented in the original aspect ratio; presented in letterbox orientation.
A stringed musical instrument, of European origin, usually with a fretted fingerboard and six strings, played with the fingers or a plectrum (guitar pick).

Any type of musical instrument of the lute family, characterized by a flat back, along with a neck whose upper surface is in the same plane as the soundboard, with strings along the neck and parallel to the soundboard.
To play the guitar.
Easily broken or destroyed, and thus often of subtle or intricate structure.
definitions.example: He is a very fragile person and gets easily depressed.

definitions.definition: Feeling weak or easily disturbed as a result of illness.
Flirting, or seeming to flirt.
Extremely wicked.
In a deft manner; quickly and neatly in action.
A kind of coarse woolen cloth, having a thick nap or frieze.
Outfit or supplies, collectively; kit.
To bring down the size, quantity, quality, value or intensity of something; to diminish, to lower.
definitions.example: to reduce weight, speed, heat, expenses, price, personnel etc.

definitions.definition: To lose weight.
To bring to an inferior rank; to degrade, to demote.
definitions.example: Hester Prynne was shocked at the condition to which she found the clergyman reduced.

definitions.definition: To humble; to conquer; to subdue; to capture.
definitions.example: to reduce a province or a fort

definitions.definition: To bring to an inferior state or condition.
definitions.example: to reduce a city to ashes

definitions.definition: To decrease the liquid content of food by boiling much of its water off.
To add electrons / hydrogen or to remove oxygen.
To produce metal from ore by removing nonmetallic elements in a smelter.
To simplify an equation or formula without changing its value.
To express the solution of a problem in terms of another (known) algorithm.
To convert a syllogism to a clearer or simpler form
To convert to written form. (Usage note: this verb almost always appears as "reduce to writing".)
definitions.example: It is important that all business contracts be reduced to writing.

definitions.definition: To perform a reduction; to restore a fracture or dislocation to the correct alignment.
To reform a line or column from (a square).
To strike off the payroll.
To annul by legal means.
To translate (a book, document, etc.).
definitions.example: a book reduced into English

definitions.definition: A long, narrow strip of timber bent and bolted longitudinally to the ribs of a vessel, to hold them in position and give rigidity to the framework.
A ribbon.
A long, narrow strip of material used for decoration of clothing or the hair or gift wrapping.
An awareness ribbon.
An inked strip of material against which type is pressed to print letters in a typewriter or printer.
A narrow strip or shred.
definitions.example: a steel or magnesium ribbon

definitions.definition: A painted moulding on the side of a ship.
A watchspring.
A bandsaw.
(in the plural) Reins for a horse.
A bearing similar to the bend, but only one eighth as wide.
(spinning) A sliver.
A subheadline presented above its parent headline.
A toolbar that incorporates tabs and menus.
To decorate with ribbon.
To stripe or streak.
A small evergreen tree native to Sri Lanka and southern India, Cinnamomum verum or Cinnamomum zeylanicum, belonging to the family Lauraceae.
Several related trees, notably the Indonesian cinnamon (Cinnamomum burmanni) and Chinese cinnamon or cassia (Cinnamomum aromaticum or Cinnamomum cassia).
A spice from the dried aromatic bark of the cinnamon tree, either rolled into strips or ground into a powder. The word is commonly used as trade name for spices made of any of the species above.
A warm yellowish-brown color, the color of cinnamon.
Containing cinnamon, or having a cinnamon taste.
Of a yellowish-brown color.
Toasted bread.
definitions.example: I ate a piece of toast for breakfast.

definitions.definition: A proposed salutation (e.g. to say "cheers") while drinking alcohol.
definitions.example: At the reception, there were many toasts from the well-wishers.

definitions.definition: A person, group, or notable object to which a salutation with alcohol is made; a person or group held in similar esteem.
definitions.example: He was the toast of high society.

definitions.definition: (chiefly US) Something that will be no more; something subject to impending destruction, harm or injury.
definitions.example: If I ever get my hands on the guy that stole my wallet, he’s toast!

definitions.definition: Extemporaneous narrative poem or rap.
A transient, informational pop-up window.
A small brush, with firm bristles, used to clean the fingernails or to scrub the hands.
Immense extent or expanse; immensity
Huge, gigantic, very large.
Supremely good.
Of or relating to sound.
Having a speed approaching that of the speed of sound in air.
Thinking about unpleasant things that have happened or that might happen; feeling afraid and unhappy.
definitions.example: She was worried about her son who had been sent off to fight in the war.

definitions.definition: To be troubled; to give way to mental anxiety or doubt.
definitions.example: Stop worrying about your test, it’ll be fine.

definitions.definition: Disturb the peace of mind of; afflict with mental agitation or distress.
definitions.example: Your tone of voice worries me.

definitions.definition: To harass; to irritate or distress.
definitions.example: The President was worried into military action by persistent advisors.

definitions.definition: To seize or shake by the throat, especially of a dog or wolf.
definitions.example: Your dog’s been worrying sheep again.

definitions.definition: To touch repeatedly, to fiddle with.
To strangle.
A unitary council.
A unitary matrix or operator.
Having the quality of oneness.
(of a system of government or administration) That concentrates power in a single body, rather than sharing it with more local bodies.
definitions.example: a unitary authority

definitions.definition: (of an algebra) That contains an identity element.
(of a matrix or operator) Whose inverse is equal to its adjoint.
An instrument or device for domestic use, especially in the kitchen.
definitions.example: We have convenient storage for all the kitchen utensils.

definitions.definition: A useful small tool, implement, or vessel.
definitions.example: He stocked up on old-style writing utensils.

definitions.definition: Any of various large nonvenomous snakes of the genus Eunectes, found mainly in northern South America. Their length can grow to as much as 5 m (15 ft).
(by extension) A large penis.
A particular variety of stud poker.
A person who is skilled at geology.
To cause to shine briefly or intermittently.
definitions.example: He flashed the light at the water, trying to see what made the noise.

definitions.definition: To blink; to shine or illuminate intermittently.
definitions.example: The light flashed on and off.

definitions.definition: To be visible briefly.
definitions.example: The scenery flashed by quickly.

definitions.definition: To make visible briefly.
definitions.example: A number will be flashed on the screen.

definitions.definition: To briefly, and often unintentionally, expose one's naked body or underwear, or part of it, in public. (Contrast streak.)
definitions.example: Her skirt was so short that she flashed her underpants as she was getting out of her car.

definitions.definition: To show or expose an "inappropriate" part of the body to someone for humorous reasons or as an act of contempt.
To break forth like a sudden flood of light; to show a momentary brilliance.
To flaunt; to display in a showy manner.
definitions.example: He flashed a wad of hundred-dollar bills.

definitions.definition: To communicate quickly.
definitions.example: The news services flashed the news about the end of the war to all corners of the globe.

definitions.definition: To move, or cause to move, suddenly.
definitions.example: Flash forward to the present day.

definitions.definition: To telephone a person, only allowing the phone to ring once, in order to request a call back.
definitions.example: Susan flashed Jessica, and then Jessica called her back, because Susan didn't have enough credit on her phone to make the call.

definitions.definition: (of liquid) To evaporate suddenly. (See flash evaporation.)
To climb (a route) successfully on the first attempt.
To write to the memory of (an updatable component such as a BIOS chip or games cartridge).
(glassmaking) To cover with a thin layer, as objects of glass with glass of a different colour.
(glassmaking) To expand (blown glass) into a disc.
To send by some startling or sudden means.
To burst out into violence.
To perform a flash.
To release the pressure from a pressurized vessel.
To trick up in a showy manner.
To strike and throw up large bodies of water from the surface; to splash.
A sudden blazing or bursting, as of fire or water.

(roofing) Components used to weatherproof or seal roof system edges at perimeters, penetrations, walls, expansion joints, valleys, drains and other places where the roof covering is interrupted or terminated.
The process of getting rid of gaps on shelves by bringing products from the back of the shelf to the front to create a 'fuller' shelf.
The blinking of a light source, such as a light bulb or computer's cursor.
definitions.example: The incessant flashing of the neon sign kept the neighborhood awake.

definitions.definition: The exposing of one's naked body, or part of it, in public briefly (the action of the verb to flash).
(jewellery) The use of rhodium plating to temporarily enhance a ring's durability and shine.
Created by humans; artificial.
Counterfeit, fabricated, fake.
To fold in the middle, as a jackknife does.
definitions.example: The cat jackknifed in the air and landed gracefully on its feet.

definitions.definition: To cause a semi-trailer truck to fold like a jackknife in a traffic accident.
definitions.example: Before I knew what was happening, I'd jack-knifed the truck.

definitions.definition: A large town house or mansion; a grand private residence, especially in France.
An establishment that provides accommodation and other services for paying guests; normally larger than a guesthouse, and often one of a chain.
A restaurant; any dining establishment.
The letter H in the ICAO spelling alphabet.
The larger red property in the game of Monopoly, in contradistinction to houses.
The guest accommodation and dining section of a cruise ship.
(Canada, US) A large, usually metal trash receptacle designed to be hoisted up by a garbage truck in order to be emptied.
A small sailboat lacking a bowsprit, of a type found primarily in the Massachusetts area
definitions.example: We sailed our knockabout around Cape Cod.

definitions.definition: (entertainment) A slapstick comedy or comedian.
(circus) A tumbler.
Clothing suitable for rough use.
A worker habitually engaged in casual employment.
A person living in rough, violent conditions.
Boisterous
Suitable for rough use.
definitions.example: I have a knockabout cello for non-concert gigs.

definitions.definition: One who performs pranks.
A vehicle used in farms e.g. for pulling farm equipment and preparing the fields.
A truck (or lorry) for pulling a semi-trailer or trailer.
Any piece of machinery that pulls something.
An airplane where the propeller is located in front of the fuselage
(rail transportation) A British Rail Class 37 locomotive.
A metal rod used in tractoration, or Perkinism.
To prepare (land) with a tractor.
To move with a tractor beam.
To treat by means of tractoration, or Perkinism.
A reporter; a person in the profession of providing news.
To breed or reproduce with those that are related.
To breed with those that share common traits or qualities.
To produce or generate within.
To ask (about something).
To make an inquiry or an investigation.
To call; to name.
Any of several fishlike marine reptiles of the extinct order Ichthyosauria of the early Triassic to the late Cretaceous period, that had a body somewhat like a porpoise.
A type of paint with a solvent that evaporates to leave a hard, transparent, glossy film.
Anything resembling such a paint; glossy appearance.
(by extension) A deceptively showy appearance.
A passenger train, probably derived from the varnished passenger cars used at one time.
To apply varnish.
To cover up with varnish.
To gloss over a defect.
To make a judgment (on a dispute) as an arbitrator or arbiter
definitions.example: to arbitrate a disputed case

definitions.definition: To submit (a dispute) to such judgment
To assign an arbitrary value to, or otherwise determine arbitrarily.
definitions.example: We wish to show f is continuous. Arbitrate epsilon greater than zero...

definitions.definition: The act of gerrymandering.
definitions.example: By this iniquitous practice, which is known as the gerrymander, the party in a minority in each State is allowed to get only about one-half or one-quarter of its proper share of representation.

definitions.definition: A voting district skewed by gerrymandering.
definitions.example: Any citizen looking at a map of district 12 could immediately tell that it was a gerrymander because of the ridiculous way it cut across 4 counties while carving up neighborhoods in half.

definitions.definition: To divide a geographic area into voting districts in such a way as to give an unfair advantage to one party in an election.
(by extension) To draw dividing lines for other types of districts in an unintuitive way to favor a particular group or for other perceived gain.
definitions.example: The superintendent helped gerrymander the school district lines in order to keep the children of the wealthy gated community in the better school all the way across town.

definitions.definition: A great amount or number, often of people; abundance, myriad, profusion.
The mass of ordinary people; the masses, the populace.
definitions.example: Pilate, wishing to please the multitude, released Barabbas to them.

definitions.definition: Any of several large North American hares of the genus Lepus
To make a sudden, rapid movement
A serving vessel or container, typically circular in cross-section and typically higher than it is wide, with a relatively small mouth or spout, an ear handle and often a stopper or top.
The amount that a jug can hold.
Jail.
(chiefly in the plural) A woman's breasts.
A kettle.
(chiefly in the plural) A kind of large, high-powered vacuum tube.
To stew in an earthenware jug etc.
definitions.example: jugged hare

definitions.definition: To put into jail.
To utter a sound like "jug", as certain birds do, especially the nightingale.
(of quails or partridges) To nestle or collect together in a covey.
One who holds the key to a building etc. for security reasons.
The individual or organisation with access to a cryptographic key.
A tall, square, tapered, stone monolith topped with a pyramidal point, frequently used as a monument.
(of a dragonfly) To adopt the obelisk posture; to point the tip of the abdomen towards the sun.

A symbol resembling a horizontal line (–), sometimes together with one or two dots (for example, ⨪ or ÷), which was used in ancient manuscripts and texts to mark a word or passage as doubtful or spurious, or redundant; an obelisk.

A dagger symbol (†), which is used in printed matter as a reference mark to refer the reader to a footnote, marginal note, etc.; beside a person's name to indicate that the person is deceased; or beside a date to indicate that it is a person's death date; an obelisk.
To besiege; to surround with troops.
To vex, harass, or beset.
To exhaust.
Any of various plants in the family Asteraceae, including most species of Chrysothamnus and some Ericameria.
To pierce or puncture slightly.
definitions.example: John hardly felt the needle prick his arm when the adept nurse drew blood.

definitions.definition: To form by piercing or puncturing.
definitions.example: to prick a pattern for embroidery

definitions.definition: To mark or denote by a puncture; to designate by pricking; to choose; to mark.
To mark the surface of (something) with pricks or dots; especially, to trace a ship’s course on (a chart).
To run a middle seam through the cloth of a sail.
To fix by the point; to attach or hang by puncturing.
definitions.example: to prick a knife into a board

definitions.definition: To be punctured; to suffer or feel a sharp pain, as by puncture.
definitions.example: A sore finger pricks.

definitions.definition: To make or become sharp; to erect into a point; to raise, as something pointed; said especially of the ears of an animal, such as a horse or dog; and usually followed by up.
definitions.example: The dog's ears pricked up at the sound of a whistle.

definitions.definition: Usually in the form prick out: to plant (seeds or seedlings) in holes made in soil at regular intervals.
To incite, stimulate, goad.
To urge one's horse on; to ride quickly.
To affect with sharp pain; to sting, as with remorse.
To make acidic or pungent.
To become sharp or acid; to turn sour, as wine.
To aim at a point or mark.
Usually as prick up: to dress or adorn; to prink.
Punctured by small holes
A small and rather deep collection of (usually) fresh water, as one supplied by a spring, or occurring in the course of a stream; a reservoir for water.
definitions.example: the pools of Solomon

definitions.definition: A small body of standing or stagnant water; a puddle.
A supply of resources.
definitions.example: There is a limited pool of candidates from which to choose the new manager.

definitions.definition: (by extension) A set of resources that are kept ready to use.
A small amount of liquid on a surface.
definitions.example: a pool of blood

definitions.definition: A localized glow of light.
(of a liquid) To form a pool.
A pool of water used for swimming, usually one which has been artificially constructed.
A bed or cot for a baby, oscillating on rockers or swinging on pivots.
The place of origin, or in which anything is nurtured or protected in the earlier period of existence.
definitions.example: a cradle of crime

definitions.definition: Infancy, or very early life.
definitions.example: from the cradle to the grave

definitions.definition: An implement consisting of a broad scythe for cutting grain, with a set of long fingers parallel to the scythe, designed to receive the grain, and to lay it evenly in a swath.
A tool used in mezzotint engraving, which, by a rocking motion, raises burrs on the surface of the plate, so preparing the ground.

A framework of timbers, or iron bars, moving upon ways or rollers, used to support, lift, or carry ships or other vessels, heavy guns, etc., as up an inclined plane, or across a strip of land, or in launching a ship.
A case for a broken or dislocated limb.
A frame to keep the bedclothes from contact with the sensitive parts of an injured person.
A machine on rockers, used in washing out auriferous earth.
A suspended scaffold used in shafts.
A ribbing for vaulted ceilings and arches intended to be covered with plaster.
A basket or apparatus in which, when a line has been made fast to a wrecked ship from the shore, the people are brought off from the wreck.
definitions.example: The cradle was ill-made. One victim fell into the sea and was lost and the ensuing delay cost three more lives.

definitions.definition: A rest for the receiver of a telephone, or for certain computer hardware.
definitions.example: He slammed the handset into the cradle.

definitions.definition: (contact juggling) A hand position allowing a contact ball to be held steadily on the back of the hand.
A mechanical device for tilting and decanting a bottle of wine.
To contain in or as if in a cradle.
To rock (a baby to sleep).
To wrap protectively, to hold gently and protectively.
definitions.example: cradling the injured man’s head in her arms

definitions.definition: To lull or quieten, as if by rocking.
To nurse or train in infancy.
To rock the lacrosse stick back and forth in order to keep the ball in the head by means of centrifugal force.
To cut and lay (grain) with a cradle.
To transport a vessel by means of a cradle.
To put ribs across the back of (a picture), to prevent the panels from warping.
